title: aconitum napellus subsp. lusitanicum
intro: Aconitum napellus subsp. lusitanicum, also known as Monk's Hood or Lusitanian Monk's Hood, is a subspecies of the Aconitum napellus plant, a perennial flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects due to the presence of alkaloids such as aconitine and delphinine. The health benefits associated with Aconitum napellus subsp. lusitanicum include its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and ointments, which should be used cautiously and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to the plant's toxicity.
